marketState: Neutral, fairly valued, with bullish cross-currents (weather, crowded COT shorts) fighting a bearish storage overhang
summary: Front-month NG is trading almost exactly at fair value ($2.885 vs $2.923 fair, status FAIR), so there is no clean mispricing to trade off the band alone. Seasonality is mildly bearish (price -8.4pp vs median, 3-4wk up-probability only 38%) but flagged Low reliability, so it carries little weight. Weather demand is running hot vs the 5-year norm (+4.7% days 1-7, +15.4% days 8-16, current demand +10% vs 5y normal) which is supportive into peak cooling season, while storage is bearish (+180 Bcf vs 5y, this week's build beat forecast by +22 Bcf). Managed-money is net short at only the 8th percentile of its 5-year range, a crowded positioning that raises short-covering-squeeze risk, and the fair-value curve itself drifts higher through +21 days. Technicals are split by horizon: intraday (5m/15m) is bullish while 4h/1d/1w trend remains bearish with RSI in the mid-30s, so there's no consensus technical edge for a fast trade right now.

longTerm.sizing: 1R approx 1% account risk; consider partial size (~0.5R) given Low seasonality reliability and bearish storage backdrop
longTerm.rationale: Managed-money net-short sits at the 8th percentile of its 5-year range (squeeze risk), demand_8_16D is running +15.4% above the 5-year norm heading into peak cooling season, and the fair-value curve itself rises from $2.923 now to $3.022 by +21D — all supportive. This is tempered by a genuinely bearish storage picture (+180 Bcf vs 5y, bearish build surprise) and a weak/low-reliability seasonality read.
longTerm.invalidation: Sustained close below $2.60, storage surprises stay bearish and weather demand rolls back under the 5-year norm, or managed-money shorts unwind without any upward price reaction.

shortTerm.rationale: Price is sitting essentially on top of fair value ($2.885 vs $2.923, spread only -$0.038, status FAIR) so there is no mispricing edge, and technicals are split by timeframe (5m/15m bullish, 1h neutral, 4h/1d/1w bearish with RSI mid-30s). The futures curve shows only a small front-vs-next backwardation (-$0.033), not enough to lean hard either way.
shortTerm.trigger: Wait for either (a) a pullback to $2.70-$2.75 (near fair-value-band low support) to buy the bounce, (b) a daily close below $2.75 confirming the 4h/1d/1w bearish trend for a short, or (c) a daily close above $3.00 with EMA/MACD flipping bullish on 1h/4h for a momentum long.

keyDrivers: Managed-money net short at 8th percentile of 5y range - crowded short, squeeze risk
Weather demand +15.4% vs 5y for days 8-16, current demand +10% vs 5y normal
Storage +180 Bcf vs 5y with a bearish +22 Bcf build surprise this week
Price essentially at fair value ($2.885 vs $2.923 fair) while the fair-value curve itself rises through +21D
Technicals conflicted: bullish intraday (5m/15m) vs bearish daily/weekly trend and RSI in mid-30s
risks: Seasonality reliability flagged Low (59pp 10-90% spread) - weak statistical edge behind the bearish seasonal tilt
